packets.claim: declaration matchers match a declaration LINE, not a prefix
packets.summary: import pdb;pdb.post_mortem() / multi-declarator require / require 'x'; File.open — all classify

packets.claim: 'call the existing helper' is safe advice
packets.summary: helper in test code recommended to prod copies; generated-file helper recommended

packets.claim: scan throughput is file-parallel
packets.summary: two 4.8k-line files serialize normalize+extract: 3.1s vs 0.63s for 1,364 files

packets.claim: presentation passes are not super-linear
packets.summary: declaration classifier re-read files per member (246 reads on fixture)

packets.claim: the clamp law converges its documented forms end-to-end
packets.summary: five escalating probes ALL refuted by sound gates (NaN typing, bound proof, strict-exact eligibility); explains LawPack zero field provenance

packets.claim: require lines are bare declarations
packets.summary: require 'x' if expensive_check() rides a condition expression

packets.claim: open multi-line declaration interiors hold only specifiers
packets.summary: os.Exit(1)) closes a Go import; } || x(); closes a JS import; require 'fs' + 1; #include <stdio.h> int x = 1; — tree-sitter error tolerance voids the parsed-file assumption
packets.defense: interior validation + strict closers + lone string args (PR #273)

packets.claim: import-list names can smuggle code
packets.summary: `eval,` inside from-import parens is still an import name — wiring, not code

packets.claim: common declaration shapes fail open (lost filtering)
packets.summary: Python docstrings / multi-line block comments inside spans fail open; requires identical comments in both members

packets.claim: hints never bypass the high-parameter caution
packets.summary: 'call the existing helper' early return skipped the params>=6 caution

packets.claim: grouping/hints misbehave on degenerate inputs
packets.summary: identical-span double families and repeated in-family locations cannot occur (upstream invariants); transitive chaining accepted §BZ; helper visibility is judgment-axis; witness-kind set verified closed by S2-C5

packets.claim: the declaration battery covers what the code supports
packets.summary: 7 supported-but-untested shapes adopted as fixtures (pub(crate) use, aliased Go import, single-line from-import, require('json'), #include<no-space>, import{ no-space, ASI import); multilingual e2e deferred

packets.claim: review --fail fires only on provable shared-line contact
packets.summary: 10 packets, zero violations; capped spot lists and first-sibling selection are fail-closed (under-fire only); insertion boundary arithmetic correct

packets.claim: scan JSON emission matches the documented contract exactly
packets.summary: two-direction audit clean; one stale checked-in example fixture (missing declaration key) refreshed; checker strengthened

packets.claim: defenses never cost corpus recall
packets.summary: bare-`)` strict closer leaked 4 real parenthesized Python imports (py 254->250); caught by the label-join re-price, closer refined to module-list+`)`

packets.claim: single-line import/export wiring is pure wiring
packets.summary: import/export { x } from Math.max("a","b"); / from x import max("a","b") / import java.util.x + y; — 6 self-verified packets with family ids; from-clause sources, Python name lists, Java paths were shape-checked only
packets.defense: from-source + specifier + name-list + dotted-path validation (series-3 PR)

packets.claim: --cache-dir scans behave identically to non-cached scans
packets.summary: build_units_cached skips the corpus-level resolve_imported_immutable_bindings pass (frontend lib.rs:195); 6 attacker probes + 2 assessor probes (imported-binding vs literal, default and semantic modes) could NOT expose an output divergence
packets.defense: #275 ESCALATED: reproduced (cold 1 exact family vs cached 0) via a converging shape mined from equivalence.rs — the test suite as discriminating-input arsenal

packets.claim: production code is NEVER misclassified as test (is_test_loc doc)
packets.summary: prod validator named test_data_loader.py and OpenAPI spec/ dir tag as test — ecosystem conventions win by design; the NEVER wording was the violation
packets.defense: doc claim softened (conventions win; scope is display context); markers unchanged

packets.claim: --scope filters prod/test/mixed across formats and --fail-on
packets.summary: verified across json/markdown/sarif + exit codes

packets.claim: structured ignores never hide members their selector does not cover
packets.summary: paths:[vendor/**] suppressed a family whose other copy lives in src/ — first-party duplication silently passed --fail-on any (any-member matching was even documented); switched to ALL-members selector semantics
packets.defense: all-members matching in ignores.rs + doc + gate test (series-3 PR)

packets.claim: a baselined family stays hidden; runs flag only NEW duplication
packets.summary: 5 packets: edits above a clone / renames / --mode switches re-key families and resurface them as new (loud, safe direction; key shape deliberate); family_id ignores share the key so they drift too
packets.defense: doc fences in continuous-integration.md (span/path/mode-sensitive keys, pin --mode, re-baseline after refactors)

packets.claim: baseline suppression, third-copy detection, ignore expiry
packets.summary: unchanged rerun hides; third copy trips the gate; expiry applied/expired both correct

packets.claim: the new series-2 helpers are covered
packets.summary: 12 gaps; adopted 7 fixture rows (Go ./_ aliases, Rust nested braces, JsTs $-idents, multiline export-from closer, require('lib') with $, from-import-*, Dict as D) + params==6/5 boundary tests + 2 closer rejection rows

packets.claim: executable-packet attackers can reproduce
packets.summary: an Explore-type attacker refused the executable contract (read-only self-interpretation); re-spawned as general-purpose with /tmp-only write scope — runbook should name the agent capability requirement

packets.claim: wave counting: a 3rd+ hardening wave demands a generalization-level escalation
packets.summary: four text-grammar waves replaced by nose_frontend::declaration_facts (tree-sitter node facts + code-poison pass); 474 lines of matchers deleted; the 47-row battery carried over unchanged; EOF-newline and end-column-0 over-cover caught by the battery during migration

packets.claim: few-huge-files serialization is worth core work
packets.summary: synthetic-only; real corpus worst cases run seconds at healthy parallelism; revisit condition recorded (real repo >10s at <200% CPU)

packets.claim: LawPack expansion is justified by axis breadth
packets.summary: exact-channel law provenance is structurally measure-zero; product role = near influence + proof discipline; Phase 3+ gated on a priced consumer case (roadmap entry gate added)

packets.claim: the AST migration is cost-neutral
packets.summary: A/B vs pre-AST binary: sympy 4.96->6.42s (+29%), craken-agents 0.546->0.760s (+39%) — serial re-parse of nearly every family-hosting file. Defense: sound-direction prescreen (declaration starters + mid-statement continuation shapes; false negatives only fail open) + parallel parse of unique candidate files. After: sympy 4.67s, craken-agents 0.550s — at or under the pre-AST baseline, classification byte-identical (42/10). The prescreen's first draft dropped a mid-statement-start family (caught by classification diff, not timing) — even perf-only gates need the classification diff check

packets.claim: a declaration node proves its whole line range is wiring
packets.summary: 4 reproduced: call-shaped declaration entries (JS require-binding, Ruby require) mark the node and early-return WITHOUT recursing, so a destructuring default `{a=steal()}`, computed key `{[exfil()]:x}`, or Ruby block `require('x'){launch}` smuggles a call onto the import line; node-kind whitelist never inspected the name-pattern/block child
packets.defense: subtree_executes guard on JS binding name + Ruby block-field check (this PR)

packets.claim: N of M lines shared is the representative pair's invariant count; honest
packets.summary: 6 packets: shared_lines was a majority vote across up to 8 members but displayed against the rep pair (5 of 6 shared + 2 spots = 7 in a 6-line body), and HashSet-deduped (3 identical buf.append counted once -> 2 of 6). Fixed display to the rep pair's physical invariant count (rank set keeps majority vote for shared_weight). Remaining packets (params from one pair = lower bound; languages==1 gate drops mixed-family evidence; removable on 0-shared) recorded as documented lower-bound behavior

packets.claim: encoding never changes classification; determinism holds
packets.summary: a UTF-8 BOM on any member of an import-only family flipped it from declaration to default (tree-sitter error leaf in line-1 region) in py+rust; the IL-lowering path already tolerated it. Determinism/CRLF/multibyte/no-trailing-newline all GREEN (3 packets BOM only)
packets.defense: strip BOM in declaration_facts + prescreen (this PR)

packets.claim: embedded <script> extraction is correct and line-accurate
packets.summary: 5 reproduced: </script> in a JS string truncates (miss); commented-out <script> analyzed live + span swallows </body></html>; generic="T extends X<Y>" attribute > breaks tag-end; end_line over-claims onto the close tag; unclosed <script> dropped. All from text byte-scanning, not parsing
packets.defense: #280 (grammar-driven boundary detection is frontend core work)

packets.claim: the AST classifier's per-language kinds are covered
packets.summary: 14 gaps; adopted rows for Rust extern crate, Go package clause, Ruby require_relative; AST code-poison (gap 6) and the languages all-members selector (gap 12) already locked by the migration + the partial-path test sharing the same .all() code path

packets.claim: equal fingerprint ⟹ equal behavior
packets.summary: FALSE MERGE (cardinal sin): print(a)+print(b) ≡ print(b)+print(a), AC chains, *, ^ — verify-confirmed; node-multiset fingerprint blind to operand order of a commutative op, effectful calls in value position not emitted as ordered effect sinks

packets.claim: type-gated rewrites are sound because the type is PROVEN
packets.summary: FALSE MERGE: -(-a)≡a, a&a≡a, a|a≡a — value domain infers Number from the op itself, so the gate passes for untyped params; differs on list input; verify canon-preservation violation

packets.claim: missing type evidence blocks exact acceptance
packets.summary: FALSE MERGE (oracle-blind): a+b≡b+a and (a+b)+c≡a+(b+c) for untyped params admitted to exact channel + commuted/associated; wrong for strings/floats; verify can't witness (battery never feeds 2 distinct strings, models i64)

packets.claim: verify declares behavior-equal only when truly equal
packets.summary: the soundness net has holes: py % (floored) ≡ js % (truncated), py / ≡ ruby / , js (x|0)+1 ≡ x+1; interp maps every Op to one Rust i64 op; index-store mutation dropped+faked as effect. Masks cross-language false merges

packets.claim: behaviorally-equal forms converge
packets.summary: 4 oracle-confirmed recall misses: abs(abs x)≡abs x and ~(a&b)≡~a|~b (both fully sound, no gate needed — worth adding); max(max(a,b),c)≡max(a,max(b,c)) (compositional: MIN/MAX in is_commutative but not is_assoc_comm_code — clean e-graph-ledger trigger); x+x≡x*2 (documented §BA gap)
packets.defense: #284 (sound recall rules: abs-idem, bitwise-DeMorgan, min/max AC)

packets.claim: canon rules are proven or tested
packets.summary: 15 gaps: byte-pack u16/u32 + low-bit-toggle have NO Lean proof (positive-only tests); many type-gated rules (abs, minmax, idempotence, negation-distrib) have positive tests but NO hard-negative proving the gate holds — the AC-chain hard-negative gap is highest risk and overlaps the #283 cardinal-sin cluster
